forecasts.reasoning: John Morgan laid out a campaign blueprint and teased running for governor of Florida in early 2025. However, there is no evidence that he formally announced his candidacy before July 1, 2025. In fact, articles published in December 2025 still describe him as 'eyeing' a run or state that he 'may run,' which clearly indicates he had not formally announced by that point. Additionally, Florida statute required him to register with a new party by June 12, 2025 if he was to run as a third-party candidate under it. The lack of any headlines declaring he formally announced before the July 1, 2025 deadline brings the probability close to 0.

The search results provide a comprehensive timeline of events through September 2025. In April 2024, the Bulgarian parliament tasked its energy minister with renegotiating the 13-year gas deal with BOTAŞ due to its severe financial toll. Throughout mid-2024 and 2025, Bulgarian officials, including Energy Minister Zhecho Stankov, consistently maintained that the agreement lacks a termination clause and that unliterally terminating it would incur a penalty of over BGN 4 billion. By May 2025, officials reiterated that the contract could not simply be terminated. In July 2025 (after the resolution date), prosecutors launched an investigation into the initial signing of the deal, and BOTAŞ representatives were in Sofia for further renegotiation talks. In September 2025, officials were still decrying the agreement as active, noting that it was decapitalizing Bulgargaz while BOTAŞ continued to insist on the full contractual payments. Since both parties treated the agreement as fully active and in renegotiation (rather than cancelled/terminated) throughout and past the July 1, 2025 deadline, the question firmly resolves as No.

Authoritative sources tracking AfCFTA ratification progress confirm that Sudan did not ratify the agreement before July 1, 2025. According to data from Tralac (Trade Law Centre) updated as of December 2025, 49 out of 54 signatory countries had formally deposited their instruments of ratification with the African Union Commission. Sudan had "approved ratification" domestically but had not yet deposited its instrument, meaning it had not officially ratified the agreement (and would be the 50th country to do so once deposited). Another source from April 2025 confirming data as of February 2025 also lists Sudan in a table of countries that "have signed but not yet ratified" the AfCFTA agreement. Additionally, Sudan has been engulfed in a severe, wide-scale civil war since April 2023, making the completion of international treaty deposits practically difficult and less of a priority. Because the formal deposit of the instrument of ratification—which constitutes ratification in international law and for the AU—had not occurred by December 2025 (well past the July 2025 deadline), this resolves definitively as a No.
